summary: The project has resolved 81 of 118 tracked issues (69%), with nearly all problem lists fully closed except three active fronts in first_proof_1 (q4, q5, q6). All 37 open issues are concentrated in q4 (22), q5 (14), and q6 (1), and no issues are currently in-progress, indicating a temporary stall in active work despite low solvability scores across most proofs.
problems: q4 carries 22 open issues — the single largest blocker — yet a low 16% solvability score, suggesting structural difficulty rather than easy cleanup.
q5 has 14 open issues with the second-lowest solvability score (14%), making it the most stuck high-volume problem.
Zero issues are in-progress system-wide, signaling no active resolution work despite 37 open items.
Solvability scores are broadly low (q8 at 9%, q3 at 13%, q5 at 14%), indicating most proofs remain far from completion.
Best-proof attempts uniformly stall at 100 issues each on a single model (claude_opus_5-4), suggesting a hard ceiling with no model diversity in the strongest runs.
highlights: 81 issues resolved overall, including complete closure of all aim_problem_lists, erdos_problems, and first_proof_2 entries.
q7 (58%) and q10 (57%) show dramatically higher solvability than peers and are the clearest near-term wins.
q6 is nearly complete with only 1 open issue against 15 resolved.
Daily resource usage is modest and efficient ($0.19 across 2 runs, ~4.8K total tokens), leaving ample budget headroom.

suggested_todos.title: Prioritize closing q6's single remaining open issue to fully resolve another problem
Launch active runs on q7 and q10 to capitalize on their high (~57-58%) solvability scores
Triage the 22 open issues in q4 to separate structural blockers from quick fixes given its low solvability
Investigate why zero issues are in-progress and re-queue work to restart active resolution
Experiment with alternative models on stuck proofs (q8, q3, q5) to break the 100-issue ceiling
Increase run frequency given low daily cost and significant remaining budget capacity
